code: lfs_pumf <- get_pumf("LFS", refresh="auto") unemployment_stats <- lfs_pumf |>    filter(LFSSTAT !="Not in labour force") |>   filter(AGE_12 %in% c("25 to 29 years","30 to 34 years", "35 to 39 years")) |>   mutate(jd=case_when(is.na(DURJLESS) ~ "Not applicable",                       DURJLESS<12 ~ "Less than one year",                       TRUE ~ "One year or more")) |>   add_lfs_SURVDATE() |>   summarize(Count=sum(FINALWT),.by=c(SURVDATE,jd,AGE_12)) |>   mutate(Share=Count/sum(Count),.by=c(SURVDATE,AGE_12)) |>   filter(jd!="Not applicable")   unemployment_stats |>   ggplot(aes(x=SURVDATE,y=Share,colour=AGE_12)) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~jd) +   scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent_format()) +   labs(title="Unemployment by duration of unemployment",        y="Unemployment rate",x=NULL,        colour="Age group",        caption="StatCan LFS (PUMF)") #> Warning: Missing values are always removed in SQL aggregation functions. #> Use `na.rm = TRUE` to silence this warning #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours. microbenchmark::microbenchmark(collect(unemployment_stats)) |>    boxplot() #> Warning in microbenchmark::microbenchmark(collect(unemployment_stats)): less #> accurate nanosecond times to avoid potential integer overflows lfs_pumf |>    filter(LFSSTAT !="Not in labour force") |>   add_lfs_SURVDATE() |>   add_lfs_GENDER_SEX() |>   summarise(Count=sum(FINALWT),.by=c(SURVDATE,LFSSTAT,GENDER_SEX)) |>   mutate(Share=Count/sum(Count),.by=c(SURVDATE,GENDER_SEX)) |>   filter(LFSSTAT=="Unemployed") |>   ggplot(aes(x=SURVDATE,y=Share,colour=GENDER_SEX)) +   geom_line() +   scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent_format()) +   labs(title="Unemployment sex/gender",        y="Unemployment rate",x=NULL,        colour="Gender",        caption="StatCan LFS (PUMF)") lfs_pumf |> close_pumf()

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: pumf_locate_or_download() ensures version directory exists extracted content parsing begins. Cache layout: Decision sequence: refresh = TRUE — delete .duckdb file(s) metadata/ subdirectory, leaving zip extracted content untouched. Stages 2 3 re-run without re-downloading. redownload = TRUE — wipe entire version directory first, proceed first-time run. Implies refresh. Already extracted? — version_is_extracted() returns TRUE subdirectory (metadata/) non-zip non-duckdb file present. TRUE, zip step skipped even zip still disk. Download — URL looked list_canpumf_collection(). Surveys distributed via Statistics Canada’s EFT portal marker "(EFT)" instead URL; function stops instructions deposit zip manually. Extract — robust_unzip() handles two edge cases: Naming collision: zips contain single top-level directory name archive (e.g. 2025-CSV.zip/). colliding directory renamed strip .zip moved version directory. Encoding: older StatCan zips store filenames without UTF-8 flag (General Purpose Bit Flag bit 11). grep/sub calls zip entry names use useBytes = TRUE avoid “invalid locale” warnings.
code: <cache_path>/   <series>/     <version>/       <original>.zip          # retained after extraction       <extracted dirs>/       metadata/               # written by Stage 2       <series>_<version>.duckdb

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: Handles single-file SPSS format used Census (2001–2021), SFS 1999, SHS, others. file typically contains DATA LIST, VARIABLE LABELS, VALUE LABELS, sometimes MISSING VALUES FORMATS sections. VARIABLE LABELS optional (e.g. Census 2011 individuals omits ). Older releases like SFS 1999 DATA LIST label sections — produce fully importable table raw codes human-readable factor levels. Key parsing details: Column ranges — DATA LIST ranges may spaces either side dash (129-135, 129 - 135, 129-  135). three normalised regex (\\d+)\\s*-\\s*(\\d+) tokenisation. Record-group marker — leading / first variable line (e.g. /PROVP  1-2) stripped, discarded, variable retained. Section terminator — DATA LIST section ends first blank line, . line, occurrence VARIABLE LABELS, VALUE LABELS, MISSING VALUES, FORMATS, EXECUTE start line. keyword check reliable terminator older files (e.g. 1991 XMF) blank line DATA LIST VARIABLE LABELS. DATA LIST type annotations — () suffix column range marks character-type variable. parser records is_char flag per column uses populate variables.csv types VARIABLE LABELS section present. Sentinel detection — variables whose VALUE LABELS sentinel phrases (“applicable”, “Valid skip”, “Don’t know”, “Data available”, etc.) classified numeric missing_low/missing_high range, character. prevents spurious NA warnings numeric values fall outside label set. Zero-padded codes — unquoted SPSS numeric codes like 01, 02 normalised via .numeric() → .character() match bare integer values CSV data. Multi-variable VALUE LABELS blocks — /VAR1 VAR2 VAR3 headers (possibly spanning continuation lines) fully parsed listed variables receive code/label pairs.

text: reading fixed-width file, row fewer two columns non-NA dropped. FWF files older StatCan archives often end \r\n\x1a (DOS EOF marker), FWF reader interprets one-character row single non-NA field; step removes silently. CSV files affected — CSV parsers handle trailing newlines correctly.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: Registry data_fixups entries applied raw character data label mapping: str_pad — left- right-pad specified columns target width. Used zero-pad codes arrive without leading zeros CSV formats (e.g. SFS). rename — rename column; applied old name present (safe surveys ship multiple release variants, e.g. Census 2021 RELIG/RELIGION_DER). cols_swap — named character vector c(= "B", C = "D") swapping pairs column names. Used surveys DATA LIST variable names transposed relative PDF documentation (e.g. WKACTMA/WKACTFA FAOCC81/MAOCC81 Census 1981 individuals). force_numeric — character vector column names treat numeric regardless many VALUE LABELS declared. Used variable integer index SPSS file mis-classifies categorical (e.g. SUBSAMPL Census 1971). codes_supplement — named list data.frames injecting code-label rows absent SPSS command files. data frame columns val, label_en, label_fr. Setting label_en = NA marks value intentionally missing (produces silent NA factor entry without warning, without introducing spurious factor level). entries verified override ledger (tests/testthat/override_verification.csv). na_values — character vector raw string sentinels become NA numeric columns (used undeclared Census income sentinels).

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: LFS handled lfs_get_pumf() (delegated directly get_pumf() without going get_pumf_connection()). Instead one DuckDB per version, LFS versions share single <cache_path>/LFS/LFS.duckdb accumulating tables lfs_eng lfs_fra. Key differences standard pipeline: Schema evolution — new LFS version adds variable absent earlier versions, column added via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN. variable changes type (e.g. VARCHAR → ENUM), ALTER COLUMN SET DATA TYPE used. Annual supersedes monthly — annual monthly versions year loaded, annual version supersedes monthly rows year. Version tracking — lfs_versions table shared DuckDB records versions downloaded parsed, refresh = "auto" downloads new versions. Read-fast path — requested version already database, lfs_get_pumf() opens read-connection returns immediately. write lock acquired unless new data actually needs written. get_pumf() return — specific version requested, function applies dplyr::filter() SURVYEAR (SURVMNTH monthly requests) full shared table. Calling get_pumf("LFS") without version returns unfiltered table. label_pumf_columns() LFS — shared schema union loaded versions, variables introduced later years (e.g. GENDER added ~2020) absent older versions’ variables.csv. label_pumf_columns() therefore reads merges metadata every loaded version directory chronological order, -recent label winning conflicts.

text: **-memory `data.frame` `tibble`**, bootstrap weights generated entirely memory augmented data frame returned. Bootstrap weights generated rescaled bootstrap: replicate sample \(n\) rows drawn replacement; bootstrap weight row \(\) replicate \(b\) `original_weight[] * count[,b]`, `count[,b]` number times row \(\) appeared draw \(b\). **Multiple weight columns (hierarchical data):** default `bsw_table` named `weight_col` (e.g. `"pumf_bsw_wstpwgt"`), calling function twice different weight columns (e.g. household weight person weight) produces two independent BSW tables two separate views without conflict. **Connection note (DuckDB path):** calling function fully shuts DuckDB -process instance held `tbl` (write connection requires exclusive access).  input `tbl` lazy tables backed DuckDB file become invalid call.  Use returned tbl instead. **Filtered input tbls (DuckDB path):** bootstrap weights always cover complete physical survey table.  `tbl` dplyr `filter()` operations applied, captured automatically re-applied returned VIEW tbl visible rows match original subset.  operations (`select()`, `mutate()`, etc.) replayed — interfere BSW columns — apply manually returned tbl needed. **ID column (DuckDB path):** stable row identifier needed link main table BSW table.  `id_col` `NULL` (default):   * survey registry `bsw_join_key` used available (e.g.     `"PEFAMID"` SFS 2016–2023) — table modification needed.   * Otherwise `pumf_row_id` column (DuckDB `rowid`) added main     survey table.  `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` O(1); `UPDATE`     fills values O(n).

title: Generate bootstrap weights for a PUMF dataset — add_bootstrap_weights
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sfs <- get_pumf("SFS", "2019") sfs_bsw <- add_bootstrap_weights(sfs, weight_col = "WSTPWGT",                                  n_replicates = 200L, seed = 42L) bsw_info(sfs_bsw) close_pumf(sfs_bsw) } # }
